message:
feat(frankenterm/main): tick 224 workflow runner spawn uses run_with_cx + add missing WaitResult::Cancelled match arms (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

**Cross-crate caller migration** — the `frankenterm` CLI crate now
drives `WorkflowRunner::run_with_cx` (tick 223) instead of the
legacy `run` at the spawn site (L15115).

Change is minimal:

    let cx = frankenterm_core::cx::Cx::current()
        .unwrap_or_else(frankenterm_core::cx::for_request);
    workflow_runner.run_with_cx(&cx, &event_bus_clone).await;

Uses `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` — the standard
pattern already used elsewhere in main.rs (L11805) — so the runner
picks up any ambient cx installed by the runtime driver, falling
back to a fresh request-scoped cx.

**Concrete benefit**: a cx-cancel on the caller's explicit cx
(installed by the outer runtime) now bubbles through the runner
loop AND all child workflow executions currently in flight.
Previously the runner.run(...) path silently dropped cx context
at the spawn boundary.

---

Incidental fix: the frankenterm crate did not compile before this
tick. The ft-xbnl0.2.3 addition of `WaitResult::Cancelled` to
wezterm.rs left 4 exhaustiveness holes in main.rs's match arms on
`waiter.wait_for(...)` return values.

Added `Ok(WaitResult::Cancelled { reason, polls })` arms at 4
sites:
- L17398 (human send with wait-for)
- L17555 (robot wait-for command)
- L24402 (human send_text with wait-for)
- L27060 (commit with wait-for)

Each arm treats Cancelled as a timeout-like error surface since
ambient `wait_for` (not `wait_for_with_cx`) should not return
Cancelled in practice — the arms exist purely for exhaustiveness.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` now succeeds
via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).
Previously failed with 4 E0004 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
